chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُعْطِي الدَّرَاهِمَ ثُمَّ يَأْخُذُهَا بِبَلَدٍ آخَرَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 119 – The man gives the Dirhams, then takes them in another city

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/324)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/324)
text: الرَّجُلِ يَبْعَثُ بِمَالٍ إِلَى أَرْضٍ فَقَالَ الَّذِي يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَبْعَثَ بِهِ أَقْرِضْنِيهِ وَ أَنَا أُوفِيكَ إِذَا قَدِمْتَ الْأَرْضَ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Numan, from Abu Al Sabbah,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the man who wants to send goods to a land, so the one whom he want to send it with says, ‘Lend it to me and I shall return to you when I am at the land’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Nu’man from abu al-Sabbah who has said the following: “About the case of a man who wants to send goods to a land, if the one with whom he wants to send says, ‘Give this to me as a loan and I will pay you when you reach that land, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is not unlawful.’”
